name: domains
context: Configure custom domains, DNS records, and SSL certificates on Vercel projects
info: Vercel provides automatic domain management with free SSL certificates via Let's Encrypt. Add custom domains to any project via the dashboard, CLI, or API. Vercel supports apex domains, subdomains, and wildcard domains. DNS can be managed through Vercel's nameservers (recommended) or via external DNS with CNAME records. SSL certificates are provisioned and renewed automatically. Domain verification ensures ownership before routing traffic. Use `vercel domains` CLI commands or the Domains API for programmatic management. See [[vercel/deploy]] for connecting domains to deployments and [[vercel/edge#middleware]] for request processing on custom domains.
triggers: add a custom domain to vercel
set up ssl for my domain
body:
# Custom Domains

Vercel routes production traffic through custom domains with automatic SSL. Every project can have multiple domains, including apex domains (`example.com`), subdomains (`app.example.com`), and wildcards (`*.example.com`).

## Adding a Domain

### CLI

```bash
# Add domain to project
vercel domains add example.com

# Add subdomain
vercel domains add app.example.com

# List domains
vercel domains ls

# Remove domain
vercel domains rm example.com
```

### API

```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.vercel.com/v10/projects/$VERCEL_PROJECT_ID/domains" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $VERCEL_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "example.com"}'
```

## DNS Configuration

### Option 1: Vercel Nameservers (Recommended)

Point your domain's nameservers to Vercel for full DNS management:

```
ns1.vercel-dns.com
ns2.vercel-dns.com
```

This enables apex domain support, automatic DNS records, and fastest propagation.

### Option 2: External DNS with CNAME

Add a CNAME record pointing to `cname.vercel-dns.com`:

```
app    CNAME    cname.vercel-dns.com
```

Note: CNAME records cannot be set on apex domains (DNS limitation). Use Vercel nameservers or a DNS provider that supports CNAME flattening (Cloudflare, Route53) for apex domains.

## SSL Certificates

Vercel automatically provisions and renews SSL certificates via Let's Encrypt for all custom domains. Certificates are issued within minutes of domain verification. Wildcard certificates are supported when using Vercel nameservers.

| Feature | Vercel NS | External DNS |
|---------|-----------|---------------|
| Apex domain | Yes | Requires CNAME flattening |
| Subdomains | Yes | Yes (CNAME) |
| Wildcard | Yes | No |
| Auto-renewal | Yes | Yes |
| Provisioning time | < 1 min | 1-10 min |

## Domain Verification

Vercel verifies domain ownership before routing traffic. Verification methods:

1. **Nameserver** — point NS records to Vercel (automatic verification)
2. **DNS TXT record** — add a `_vercel` TXT record with the provided verification token
3. **CNAME** — the CNAME record itself serves as verification for subdomains

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| Domain not resolving | Check DNS propagation (allow 24-48h for NS changes) |
| SSL not provisioning | Verify domain ownership, check CAA records |
| 404 on custom domain | Ensure domain is assigned to the correct project |
| Redirect loop | Check middleware and `vercel.json` redirects |
| Apex not working | Switch to Vercel nameservers or use CNAME flattening |

sections.title: Adding domains
sections.body: Add domains via `vercel domains add <domain>`, the project dashboard, or the Domains API. Each domain must be verified before traffic is routed. Assign multiple domains to a single project. The first domain added becomes the primary (canonical) domain. Use `vercel domains inspect <domain>` to check configuration status.

sections.title: DNS configuration
sections.body: Vercel nameservers (`ns1.vercel-dns.com`, `ns2.vercel-dns.com`) provide the simplest setup with apex domain support and automatic records. For external DNS, add a CNAME to `cname.vercel-dns.com` for subdomains. Apex domains with external DNS require CNAME flattening (Cloudflare CNAME, Route53 ALIAS). Manage DNS records with `vercel dns` CLI commands when using Vercel nameservers.

sections.title: SSL certificates
sections.body: SSL certificates are provisioned automatically via Let's Encrypt after domain verification. Certificates renew before expiration with no manual intervention. Wildcard certificates (`*.example.com`) are supported with Vercel nameservers. Check certificate status in the project dashboard or via `vercel certs ls`. If provisioning fails, verify that CAA DNS records (if present) allow `letsencrypt.org`.

sections.title: Wildcard domains
sections.body: Wildcard domains (`*.example.com`) route all subdomains to the project. Requires Vercel nameservers. Wildcard SSL certificates are automatically provisioned. Use [[vercel/edge#middleware]] to route wildcard subdomains to different content (e.g., `tenant1.example.com` to tenant-specific pages). Specific subdomain records take priority over wildcards.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.body: Common issues: DNS propagation delay (wait 24-48h after NS changes), SSL provisioning failure (check CAA records, verify domain ownership), 404 errors (verify project assignment), redirect loops (check [[vercel/edge#middleware]] and `vercel.json` rewrites). Use `vercel domains inspect` and `dig` to diagnose DNS issues. Check `https://vercel.com/projects/<project>/domains` for real-time status.
